---
title: "Lunaland"
subtitle: "Scientific data interpretation: how messy biological data moves through analysis, uncertainty, and communication."
---

Lunaland is a portfolio ecosystem about turning complex biological data into interpretable, usable insight.

It connects workflow development, statistical interpretation, bioinformatics judgment and science communication into one coherent story.

## Explore

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [Projects](projects/index.qmd)

Reproducible workflows, Shiny apps and practical data interpretation tools.

**Projects show what I can build.**
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [Bioinformatics Pitfalls Lab](pitfalls/index.qmd)

Small practical modules about common mistakes in bioinformatics analysis.

**The Pitfalls Lab shows my judgment.**
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [The Crosswalk](crosswalk/index.qmd)

Short field notes from the intersections of biology, data, statistics and curiosity.

**Field Notes show how I think.**
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [Concept Atlas](atlas/index.qmd)

A lightweight map of recurring concepts across the portfolio.

**The Atlas shows how ideas connect.**
:::

:::

## The organizing idea

Biological data rarely moves cleanly from experiment to conclusion. It passes through quality control, assumptions, metadata, uncertainty, statistical modeling and communication.

This site documents that path.
